tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

define void @test_self_referential_phi(i1 %cond) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %a = phi float [ 1.0, %entry ], [ %a, %loop ]
  br i1 %cond, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"

define void @test_self_referential_phi(i1 %cond) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  br i1 %cond, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary describes PHI webs as interconnected chains between multiple PHIs, but doesn't mention the edge case of a single PHI directly referencing itself. This is a degenerate web of size 1 that the worklist algorithm still handles.

tests.source_program:
define void @test_triangular_phi_cycle(i1 %cond) {
entry:
  br label %BB1

BB1:
  %a = phi i32 [ %c, %BB3 ], [ 0, %entry ]
  br i1 %cond, label %BB2, label %exit

BB2:
  %b = phi i32 [ %a, %BB1 ]
  br i1 %cond, label %BB3, label %exit

BB3:
  %c = phi i32 [ %b, %BB2 ]
  br label %BB1

exit:
  ret void
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define void @test_triangular_phi_cycle(i1 %cond) {
entry:
  br label %BB1

BB1:
  br i1 %cond, label %BB2, label %exit

BB2:
  br i1 %cond, label %BB3, label %exit

BB3:
  br label %BB1

exit:
  ret void
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary describes linear chain generalization but doesn't explicitly cover triangular/n-way mutual cycles where 3+ PHIs form a closed loop (A->B->C->A) rather than a linear chain

alive2.src:
define void @test_triangular_phi_cycle(i1 %cond) {
entry:
  br label %BB1

BB1:
  %a = phi i32 [ %c, %BB3 ], [ 0, %entry ]
  br i1 %cond, label %BB2, label %exit

BB2:
  %b = phi i32 [ %a, %BB1 ]
  br i1 %cond, label %BB3, label %exit

BB3:
  %c = phi i32 [ %b, %BB2 ]
  br label %BB1

exit:
  ret void
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define void @test_triangular_phi_cycle(i1 %cond) {
entry:
  br label %BB1

BB1:                                              ; preds = %BB3, %entry
  br i1 %cond, label %BB2, label %exit

BB2:                                              ; preds = %BB1
  br i1 true, label %BB3, label %exit

BB3:                                              ; preds = %BB2
  br label %BB1

exit:                                             ; preds = %BB2, %BB1
  ret void
}

tests.source_program:
define void @test_phi_web_with_constant_sources(i1 %cond) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:
  %x = phi i64 [ 42, %entry ], [ %y, %back ]
  %y = phi i64 [ 100, %entry ], [ %x, %back ]
  br i1 %cond, label %back, label %exit

back:
  br label %loop

exit:
  ret void
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define void @test_phi_web_with_constant_sources(i1 %cond) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:
  br i1 %cond, label %back, label %exit

back:
  br label %loop

exit:
  ret void
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on PHI def-use chains but doesn't mention that dead PHI webs can have constant incoming values alongside PHI references from the web
